title: E6.T3: Package as .skill archive (optional, backlog)
description: If skill-creator package_skill.py is available, package the skill as a .skill file. Otherwise document the manual packaging process (tar/zip archive of the directory). This is a backlog item - low priority.

title: E7.T1: Lore consistency review
description: Review all files for lore consistency: SKILL.md and README.md use Gas Town voice appropriately, reference files are technical prose (no gratuitous lore in working documents), script --help output is professional (no lore in tool interfaces), error messages may use brief Wasteland idioms but must be primarily informative. Lore enhances, never obscures.

title: E2.T5: Write references/security-checklist.md
description: Document security considerations for skill authoring and distribution. Must cover: script execution risks, prompt injection via skill content, memory poisoning (skills modifying SOUL.md/MEMORY.md), supply chain risks, mitigations (allowed-tools capability dropping, .source.json provenance, cryptographic signatures, dry-run previews), Signed Skill roadmap, practical 10-15 item yes/no checklist. Distinguish implemented vs roadmap items. Under 2000 tokens.

title: E7.T2: Create example skills in assets/examples/
description: Create two example SKILL.md files in assets/examples/: (1) minimal-skill.md - absolute minimum valid SKILL.md with name+description frontmatter and one paragraph of body text, showing minimum required; (2) full-skill.md - richly featured example showing all optional frontmatter fields, progressive disclosure, script references, reference file indexes, lore-appropriate voice. Both files must pass frontmatter validation.

title: E4.T1: Write SKILL.md final frontmatter
description: Replace placeholder frontmatter with production version. description field must: follow description-craft.md patterns, describe what skill does (orchestrates Agent Skill creation lifecycle), include >=5 explicit trigger phrases (build a skill, create a SKILL.md, validate my skill, skill for Claude Code, agentskills.io), be <=1024 chars. All fields: name, description, license, compatibility, metadata (author, version, lore-epoch, codename), allowed-tools. Pass validate_skill.py. name matches parent dir.

title: E5.T1: Self-validation pass (all tools against self)
description: Run every validation tool against the completed skill: (1) uv run scripts/validate_skill.py . --strict --json, (2) uv run scripts/audit_disclosure.py . --json, (3) uv run scripts/token_estimate.py . --json, (4) skills-ref validate . (if available). Fix all issues. Re-run until all pass. Gate: validate_skill.py --strict exits 0, audit_disclosure exits 0 or 2, token estimates within budget.

title: E1.T4: Create .gitignore and project hygiene files
description: Add .gitignore excluding __pycache__/, *.pyc, .venv/, *.egg-info/, dist/, build/, .uv/. Optionally add .editorconfig for consistent formatting (2-space YAML/MD, 4-space Python, UTF-8, LF).

title: E2.T3: Write references/script-design.md
description: Document best practices for designing scripts agents can execute reliably. Must cover: no interactive prompts, --help as primary interface docs, helpful error messages, structured output (JSON stdout / diagnostics stderr), idempotency, dry-run support, exit codes, predictable output size with pagination, PEP 723 inline metadata for Python (uv run), Deno npm: specifiers, Bun auto-install, Ruby bundler/inline, one-off commands (uvx, npx, bunx). Each pattern with rationale. Under 2000 tokens.

title: E3.T1: Build scripts/scaffold.py
description: Scaffolding script that generates a new skill directory. Interface: --name (required, kebab-case), --description (required), --license (default Apache-2.0), --compatibility, --scripts, --references, --assets, --output, --dry-run, --json, --help. Validates name/description before creating anything. Generates SKILL.md with valid frontmatter and body scaffold. PEP 723 inline deps (stdlib only). Exit codes: 0=success, 1=invalid args, 2=output dir exists. --json output is valid JSON with path and files list.

title: E1.T1: Initialize repository structure
description: Create the skill directory skeleton: empty subdirs scripts/, references/, assets/, assets/skill-template/, assets/examples/. Create placeholder SKILL.md with valid YAML frontmatter (name: gastown-admin-skill, placeholder description). Name must be kebab-case, 1-64 chars, no consecutive/leading/trailing hyphens.

title: E3.T3: Build scripts/token_estimate.py
description: Estimates token counts for each progressive disclosure tier. Output: token count for frontmatter (T1), SKILL.md body (T2), each references/ file (T3), aggregate totals, budget violations. Default: word-count heuristic (words x 1.3). Optional tiktoken for precise counts if available (PEP 723 optional dep). Interface: PATH [--model MODEL] [--json] [--help]. Must work without tiktoken. Flags T2 > 5000 token violations.

title: E5.T3: Cross-reference audit (all file refs valid)
description: Manually verify every file reference in SKILL.md: every scripts/X.py reference points to existing file, every references/X.md reference points to existing file, every 'Read when' condition in the index is accurate, every script --help output matches the interface documented in blueprint. Zero broken references. Zero stale interface descriptions.

title: E7.T3: Final validation and sign-off
description: Final gate. Run complete validation suite. Checklist: (1) validate_skill.py --strict -> exit 0, (2) audit_disclosure.py -> exit 0 or 2, (3) token_estimate.py -> all tiers within budget, (4) .source.json hashes verified, (5) README.md complete, (6) LICENSE.txt present, (7) all example files valid, (8) AGENTS.md present, (9) SKILL.md <=500 lines, (10) no broken file references. All 10 items must pass.

title: E4.T3: Write SKILL.md Prospect & Assay stage instructions
description: Write detailed instructions for Stage 1 (Prospect) and Stage 2 (Assay). Prospect: identify target capability, define >=5 semantic triggers, identify env dependencies, determine target harnesses, assess if scripts needed. Assay: design directory structure, plan progressive disclosure allocation, estimate token budgets (reference scripts/token_estimate.py), plan harness compat shims (reference references/harness-compat.md). Both stages include decision trees. Combined <120 lines. Reference specific files by relative path.

title: E1.T3: Create AGENTS.md (OpenCode shim)
description: Create AGENTS.md at skill root with OpenCode-specific tool permission declarations: edit, write, bash (scoped to git, uv, python3, skills-ref), and read. File is inert in non-OpenCode harnesses.

title: E1.T2: Add LICENSE.txt (Apache-2.0)
description: Add LICENSE.txt containing full Apache-2.0 license text at skill root. Update SKILL.md frontmatter license field to Apache-2.0.

title: E4.T7: Enforce SKILL.md 500-line budget
description: Count total lines of completed SKILL.md (wc -l SKILL.md). If >500, refactor: move largest sections to new reference files, replace with summaries and 'read references/X.md' pointers. Re-run validate_skill.py and audit_disclosure.py after refactoring. No content lost - extracted material exists in references/. SKILL.md must remain functionally complete.

title: E2.T2: Write references/harness-compat.md
description: Document cross-platform compatibility across major execution harnesses: Claude Code (.claude/skills/), GitHub Copilot (.github/skills/), OpenCode (AGENTS.md shim, opencode.json), Goose (.goose/), Aider (dual-model architecture), Amp (compaction primitives). Include path resolution hierarchy and conflict resolution. Clearly distinguish normative spec vs harness-specific extensions. Mark unverified claims [unverified]. Under 2000 tokens.

title: E2.T4: Write references/description-craft.md
description: Teach agents to write skill descriptions that trigger reliably without false positives. Must cover: dual requirement (what skill does AND when to use it), semantic trigger phrases, the pushy principle (lean toward over-triggering), the 1024-char limit, anti-patterns (too vague, too narrow, too aggressive), >=3 good and >=3 bad examples with explanations, keyword density, testing methodology. Actionable standalone reference. Under 2000 tokens.

title: E5.T2: Bootstrap test: scaffold a test skill
description: Use completed gastown-admin-skill to scaffold a trivial test skill (e.g. hello-world): (1) run scaffold.py, (2) run validate_skill.py against it, (3) run audit_disclosure.py against it, (4) run token_estimate.py against it, (5) run gen_source_json.py against it. All five scripts must execute without error. Delete test skill after verification.

title: E6.T1: Generate .source.json provenance
description: Run 'uv run scripts/gen_source_json.py .' to generate provenance file at skill root. Verify hashes by spot-checking >=3 files with sha256sum. .source.json must exist and all hashes must be correct.

title: E6.T2: Write README.md with install instructions
description: Create README.md at skill root. Contents: what skill does (brief, for humans), installation for each harness (Claude Code: .claude/skills/, GitHub Copilot: .github/skills/, OpenCode: .opencode/skills/, Goose: .goose/skills/, global: ~/.config/agents/skills/), prerequisites (Python 3.11+, uv), quick start example, link to blueprint, license. Gas Town voice for flavor OK; install instructions must be plain and precise. >=4 harnesses covered.

title: E2.T1: Write references/spec-summary.md
description: Condense the agentskills.io specification into a focused practitioner reference. Must cover: frontmatter field requirements (name, description, license, compatibility, metadata, allowed-tools) with constraints and examples, directory structure rules, progressive disclosure tiers with token budget guidance, file referencing conventions, validation command, name field regex (lowercase alphanum + hyphens, 1-64 chars), description field (1-1024 chars). Under 2000 tokens. No fabrication beyond the spec.

title: E4.T2: Write SKILL.md body: pipeline overview
description: Write opening sections of SKILL.md body: brief overview (what this skill does, what it produces, what it does NOT do), six-stage pipeline summary with stage names/inputs/outputs/gates, Quick Start path for simple skills (Prospect -> Refine -> Harden), table of contents. Must be concise: agent understands full pipeline within ~500 tokens. <100 lines. Gas Town metaphors for stage names; plain imperative English for procedural instructions.

title: E4.T5: Write SKILL.md Harden/Ship/Maintain stage instructions
description: Write instructions for Stages 4-6. Harden: run validate_skill.py --strict --json, audit_disclosure.py --json, token_estimate.py --json, skills-ref validate (if available), review security-checklist.md, fix all errors, gate=zero errors. Ship: gen_source_json.py, document install paths per harness, create README.md, optionally package as .skill. Maintain: version bump workflow, description re-optimization, regression testing, deprecation process. Ship covers >=3 distribution methods. Combined <120 lines.

title: E3.T2: Build scripts/validate_skill.py
description: Validation script checking skill dir against agentskills.io spec. Checks: SKILL.md exists, YAML frontmatter parses, name field (present, 1-64 chars, kebab-case, no consecutive hyphens, matches parent dir), description (present, 1-1024 chars), license/compatibility/metadata/allowed-tools field validity, SKILL.md body exists, line count <=500 (warning), referenced files exist, scripts have shebangs. Interface: PATH [--strict] [--json] [--help]. Exit codes: 0=pass, 1=errors, 2=warnings. --json output with valid/errors/warnings fields. Stdlib only.

title: E3.T5: Build scripts/gen_source_json.py
description: Generates .source.json provenance file recording authorship, version, creation date, SHA-256 hashes for all files. Output schema: schema_version, skill_name, author, version, created_at, files (filename -> sha256 map). Interface: PATH [--author AUTHOR] [--version VER] [--json] [--help]. Idempotent: same files = same output. Exit codes documented. Stdlib only (hashlib, json, pathlib).

title: E4.T6: Write SKILL.md scripts & references index sections
description: Add two index sections to SKILL.md body. Scripts index: list every scripts/ file with one-line description and invocation command (e.g. 'uv run scripts/X.py --help'). References index: list every references/ file with one-line description and 'Read when' condition. These are routing tables - agent knows what resources exist without loading them. No orphans: every listed file exists, every existing file is listed.

title: E4.T4: Write SKILL.md Refine stage instructions
description: Write detailed instructions for Stage 3 (Refine). Cover: 1) writing target SKILL.md frontmatter (reference description-craft.md and spec-summary.md), 2) writing target SKILL.md body (<500 lines, progressive disclosure), 3) writing scripts (reference script-design.md, PEP 723, --help/--json/exit codes), 4) writing reference files (<2000 tokens each, focused, referenced from SKILL.md), 5) running scaffold.py as first step for new skills. Tell agent when NOT to generate code. <150 lines. Relative paths for all file refs.

title: E3.T4: Build scripts/audit_disclosure.py
description: Audits skill directory for progressive disclosure QUALITY compliance. Checks: no large code blocks in SKILL.md (>30 lines in fenced block), no sections >100 lines that belong in references/, each reference file <2000 tokens, reference files referenced from SKILL.md (orphan detection), scripts referenced from SKILL.md, token budget summary per tier. Interface: PATH [--json] [--help] with exit codes. Produces actionable recommendations with line numbers.
